source: Emigration Commissioner, Malaya- correspondence with Mr. W. J. K. Stark__CO273:671:2
filename: Emigration Commissioner, Malaya- correspondence with Mr. W. J. K. Stark__CO273:671:2.txt
summary:
- **Core purpose/topic:** Colonial Office file (1942–43) on the work and status of **W. J. K. Stark** (Malayan Representative in India / Emigration Commissioner for Malaya) during wartime, covering administration of Malayan-related offices in India, staff/pay issues, auditing of accounts, and handling Malayan government/immigration assets and liabilities while Malaya was enemy-occupied.  
- **Geography & period:** Mainly **India (Bangalore, Bombay, Avadi/Madras, Negapatam, New Delhi; also Coimbatore & Ootacamund camps)** in **1942** (with some follow-up into early **1943**); references to **Malaya/Straits Settlements/Federated Malay States**, and financial correspondence involving **Burma** and **Ceylon/Colombo**.  
- **Key actors/organizations:** **W. J. K. Stark**; **Colonial Office (Secretary of State for the Colonies and officials incl. Edwin Taylor, J.J. Paskin)**; **Government of India** departments (Defence, Labour, Indians Overseas), **Government of Madras/Bombay**, **Accountant-General, Burma**; **Crown Agents for the Colonies**; **Indian Immigration Committee / Indian Immigration Fund of Malaya**; auditors **Fraser & Ross** (Madras & Bangalore).  
- **Major decisions/proposals:**  
  - Approval for Stark to **use a European firm of Chartered Accountants** to audit his office accounts; **Fraser & Ross** undertook the audit and issued a first report.  
  - Discussion/approval in principle for Stark to **sign an agreement** on behalf of the **Indian Immigration Committee** regarding depots/assets (Avadi and other depots) used by Indian Defence authorities; proposal to create a **committee in London** to manage **Indian Immigration Fund assets outside Malaya** (exact composition partly unclear due to OCR gaps).  
  - Ongoing policy handling of **Malayan government employees in India** (often treated as **leave without pay**) and maintaining a **minimum staff** at the **Negapatam depot**; Stark recommended **abolition/termination** of the **Resident Medical Officer** post at Negapatam due to lack of work.  
- **Notable events/issues:**  
  - Stark’s **transfer of headquarters to Bangalore** (office at **Andrews Building**) after release/partial release from military duties; management of two offices (Bangalore and Bombay) and liaison with evacuee/dependant payments.  
  - **Depots at Avadi/Helpakkam**: Indian Defence took possession of depots (noted as of **1/6/41**) under terms described as **rent-free**, maintenance borne by the (OCR-unclear: likely Malayan side/“Sultan” is probably misread), and restoration in existing condition; Stark pressed for clearer responsibility for properties.  
  - Handling of **cargo/valuables**: **28 packages** said to contain **Malayan currency notes/postage stamps** moved under police guard to Bangalore; separate report of **packages of valuables landed at Colombo** needing tracing of owners.  
  - Visit reports on **evacuee camps** at **Coimbatore** and **Ootacamund** (Sept 1942), including numbers of Malayan evacuees and conditions; issues around allowances and repayment of Government of India-incurred expenses.  
- **Key numbers/dates (examples):**  
  - Audit period covered **1 Nov 1941–30 Jun 1942**; auditors flagged missing receipts and specific unsupported payments (e.g., **Rs 1417-15-0** draft; **Rs 30-4-0** income tax item; deposits held for individuals **Rs 232-8-0** and **Rs 953-4-0**).  
  - Funding requests/flows: Stark reported receipts from Government of India such as **Rs 200,000** (e.g., 9 Sept 1942) and **Rs 100,000** (1 Oct 1942), and projected further needs up to **28 Feb 1943**.  
  - Staff/pay proposal: approval reported for **First Clerk, Negapatam** scale **Rs 1680–120–2400**, with present holder to receive **Rs 2200 p.a. from 1 Jan 1942** (confirmation sought from J. M. Barron).  
  - Burma/Straits Settlements account item: undeliverable demand draft for **Stg.$212.68** (dated **13 Dec 1941**) could not be refunded during hostilities per Chartered Bank instructions.
